zh.lifeSummary: 當Miles de Noyers於從1252年1月8日到1253年1月7日出生在法國布格諾也斯時，他的父親Milon VIII de Noyers39歲，母親Alixent Hélissende des Barres28歲。他和Marie de Châtillon-Crécy至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於從1291年1月8日到1292年1月7日在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Miles de Noyers于从1252年1月8日到1253年1月7日出生在法国布格諾也斯时，他的父亲Milon VIII de Noyers39岁，母亲Alixent Hélissende des Barres28岁。他和Marie de Châtillon-Crécy至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于从1291年1月8日到1292年1月7日在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Noyers
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German Meyer 1 or Mayer , with excrescent -s.
Dutch: patronymic from Moyer 3, or a variant of Moyaers, a nickname for a dandy, from a derivative of Middle Dutch moy, moeie ‘fine, handsome’.
Irish: Anglicized form of Mac an Mhaoir ‘son of the steward’, with English excrescent -s.

variants.name: Meyer
meanings: German and Swiss German: from Middle High German meier, a status name for a steward, bailiff, or overseer, which later came to be used also to denote a tenant farmer, which is normally the sense in the many compound surnames formed with this term as a second element. Originally it denoted a village headman (ultimately from Latin maior ‘greater, superior’). This form of the surname is also established in France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine); see also 3 below. Compare Maier , Mayer , Meier , and Myer .

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Meyer, from Hebrew Meir ‘enlightener’, a derivative of or ‘light’ with the prefix m-. Compare Maier , Majer , Major , Mayer , Mayor , Meier , and Meir .
Dutch: variant, also Flemish and Americanized, of   Meijer  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is also established in South Africa, where it was also brought from France (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Miles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Norman origin but uncertain derivation. Unlike most Norman names it is, as far as can be ascertained, not derived from any known Old French or Germanic name element. It may be a greatly altered pet form of Michael , which came to be associated with the Latin word miles ‘soldier’ because of the military attributes of the archangel Michael. However, the usual Latin form of the name in the Middle Ages was Milo. There is a common Slavic name element mil ‘grace, favour’, with which it may possibly have some connection. The name has been in regular use in England since the 16th century. See also Milo and Myles .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Miles de Noyers was born from 8 January 1252 to 7 January 1253, in Noyers, Yonne, Burgundy, France, his father, Milon VIII de Noyers, was 39 and his mother, Alixent Hélissende des Barres, was 28. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Marie de Châtillon-Crécy. He died from 8 January 1291 to 7 January 1292, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Miles de Noyers est né du 8 janvier 1252 au 7 janvier 1253, à Noyers, Yonne, Bourgogne, France, son père, Milon VIII de Noyers, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Alixent Hélissende des Barres, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Marie de Châtillon-Crécy. Il est décédé du 8 janvier 1291 au 7 janvier 1292, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Miles de Noyers von 8. Januar 1252 bis 7. Januar 1253 in Noyers, Yonne, Burgund, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Milon VIII de Noyers 39 und seine Mutter Alixent Hélissende des Barres 28. Er hatte mit Marie de Châtillon-Crécy mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb von 8. Januar 1291 bis 7. Januar 1292 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Miles de Noyers è nato il da 8 gennaio 1252 a 7 gennaio 1253 nel Noyers, Yonne, Bourgogne, Francia, il suo padre, Milon VIII de Noyers, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Alixent Hélissende des Barres, aveva 28 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con Marie de Châtillon-Crécy. Morì da 8 gennaio 1291 a 7 gennaio 1292, nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Miles de Noyersがから1252年1月8日まで1253年1月7日にフランスボウルゴグンイオンノイエルズで生まれた時，父Milon VIII de Noyersは39歳，母Alixent Hélissende des Barresは28歳であった。彼はMarie de Châtillon-Crécyとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼はから1291年1月8日まで1292年1月7日に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Miles de Noyers(이)가 1252년 1월 8일부터 1253년 1월 7일까지 프랑스 부르고그니 욘 노예어스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Milon VIII de Noyers(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Alixent Hélissende des Barres(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Marie de Châtillon-Crécy(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1291년 1월 8일부터 1292년 1월 7일까지 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Miles de Noyers родился с 8 января 1252 по 7 января 1253 в местности ноиирс, енн, бургогн, Франция, его отцу по имени Milon VIII de Noyers было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Alixent Hélissende des Barres, 28. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Marie de Châtillon-Crécy. Он умер с 8 января 1291 по 7 января 1292 в его родном городе.
